2605.19943.title: Probabilistic Tiny Recursive Model
2605.19943.paper_summary: Tiny Recursive Models (TRM) solve complex reasoning tasks with a fraction of the parameters of modern large language models (LLMs) by iteratively refining a latent state and final answer. While powerful, their deterministic recursion can lead to convergence at suboptimal solutions, without escape mechanism. A common workaround relies on task-specific input perturbations at test time combined with answer aggregation via voting. We introduce Probabilistic TRM (PTRM), a task-agnostic framework for test-time compute scaling that addresses this limitation through stochastic exploration. PTRM injects Gaussian noise at each deep recursion step, enabling parallel trajectories to explore diverse solution basins, and selects among them using the model's existing Q head (used for early stopping in the original TRM). Without requiring retraining or task-specific augmentations, PTRM enables substantial accuracy gains across benchmarks, including Sudoku-Extreme (87.4% to 98.75%) and on various puzzles from Pencil Puzzle Bench (62.6% to 91.2%). On the latter, PTRM achieves nearly double the accuracy of frontier LLMs (91.2% vs. 55.1%) at less than 0.0001x the cost, using only 7M parameters.
2605.19943.paper_summary_zh: 微型遞迴模型（TRM）以現代大型語言模型（LLMs）參數的極小部分解決複雜的推理任務，通過迭代地細化潛在狀態和最終答案。雖然強大，但其確定性遞迴可能導致收斂於次優解，且沒有逃脫機制。一種常見的變通方法依賴於在測試時的任務特定輸入擾動，並通過投票進行答案聚合。我們介紹了概率性TRM（PTRM），這是一種無任務特定的測試時計算擴展框架，通過隨機探索來解決這一限制。PTRM在每個深度遞迴步驟中注入高斯噪聲，使得平行軌跡可以探索多樣的解決盆地，並使用模型現有的Q頭（用於原始TRM中的早期停止）在它們之間進行選擇。PTRM無需重新訓練或任務特定的增強，能在基準測試中實現顯著的準確性提升，包括數獨極限（從87.4%提升至98.75%）以及來自鉛筆謎題基準的各種謎題（從62.6%提升至91.2%）。在後者中，PTRM的準確性幾乎是前沿LLMs的兩倍（91.2%對比55.1%），成本卻不到0.0001倍，僅使用了700萬個參數。
2605.19943.author: Amin Sghaier et.al.
2605.19943.authors: Amin Sghaier, Ali Parviz, Alexia Jolicoeur-Martineau
